content: Just as hierarchical virtual context management uses OS-inspired tiered memory to extend LLM context beyond hard physical context limits, we can reframe autonomous biological/chemical agents as naturally evolved hierarchical context managers that dynamically offload non-urgent sensory and state information to environmental 'storage' to keep their active working processing capacity uncluttered. This framing lets us translate artificial memory management innovations to design more robust long-horizon biological agents, and reverse-engineer natural agent context strategies to improve artificial hierarchical context systems.
